# Anchor: security_no_dangerous_patterns

> Source: /Users/richlabarca/LocalCode/purlin/dev/external-refs/security-policy.git
> Path: security_policy.md
> Pinned: e18cecd9932191ea24361f2c472bb2818307e4d6
> Scope: scripts/**/*.py, scripts/**/*.sh, scripts/**/*.js
> Description: Enforces the absence of dangerous code patterns across all executable Purlin framework code. The codebase is currently clean — this anchor exists to prove and maintain that state. Any feature requiring this anchor inherits these constraints.

## Rules

- RULE-1: FORBIDDEN — No `eval()` or `exec()` calls in scripts/
- RULE-2: FORBIDDEN — No `subprocess` calls with `shell=True` in scripts/
- RULE-3: FORBIDDEN — No `os.system()` calls in scripts/
- RULE-4: FORBIDDEN — No hardcoded credential assignments (password/secret/api_key/token literals) in scripts/
- RULE-5: All subprocess calls use list-mode arguments (not string commands)

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): Grep `scripts/` for `eval(` and `exec(` in `.py` files; verify zero matches
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): Grep `scripts/**/*.py` for `shell\s*=\s*True`; verify zero matches
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Grep `scripts/**/*.py` for `os\.system\s*\(`; verify zero matches
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): Grep `scripts/` for assignment patterns `(password|secret|api_key|token)\s*=\s*["'][^"']+["']` in `.py`, `.sh`, `.js` files, excluding test files and comments; verify zero matches
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): Grep `scripts/**/*.py` for `subprocess.run(` and `subprocess.call(`; verify each call's first argument is a list literal `[`, not a string @integration
